title: High-stakes-instructional-text escalation path (expert sign-off / "not advice")
context: The default pipeline explicitly excludes narrating high-stakes instructional text (medical, legal, safety procedures) as authoritative read-aloud. However, a structured escalation path is needed to define when and how such content could be narrated with appropriate framing and credentialed expert sign-off, or to codify the decline rule clearly so pipeline operators know what to do when a requested source text falls into this category. This is a policy-definition task, not a production task.
objective: Define the escalation path for high-stakes instructional text: the criteria for classifying a text as high-stakes, the required expert sign-off process and framing, and the decline rule if criteria cannot be met.
acceptanceCriteria: Defines the criteria for classifying a source text as high-stakes instructional (medical, legal, financial, safety) and requiring escalation out of the default pipeline flow.

Defines the required escalation process: which credentialed expert(s) must review and sign off (e.g. clinician for medical content, licensed attorney for legal content), the required 'not advice / not a substitute for professional guidance' framing that must accompany the read-along, and how the expert sign-off is recorded.

A subject-matter expert with credentials relevant to the text category (e.g. a clinician for medical content) reviews and approves the escalation criteria and sign-off process before this document is published.
A patient-advocate or end-user representative reviews the 'not advice' framing for clarity and appropriateness before this document is published.
States clearly that the default rule is decline: if the escalation criteria cannot be met (no credentialed expert available, no agreed framing), the text is excluded from the pipeline.
resources: PLAN.md (high-stakes instructional text escalation, risk-tier high, quality review & risk gates section)
CLAUDE.md (Elyos refusal guardrails: high-stakes advice without risk-tier expert review)
Elyos good-deed-definition.md (risk tiers and expert sign-off requirements)
output: A high-stakes escalation policy document (docs/high-stakes-escalation-policy.md), CC-BY-4.0, defining classification criteria, the expert sign-off process and 'not advice' framing requirement, required sign-offs from a credentialed domain expert and a patient-advocate or end-user representative, and the default decline rule.
